programmes.show_description: Surely royalty wouldn't lie? Danny Dyer is among the famous fibbers in the award-winning comedy panel show. Jon Richardson, Joe Lycett and Moira Stuart also adopt poker faces.
Ulex: Sandi Toksvig is joined by Bridget Christie, Rosie Jones, Cariad Lloyd and Alan Davies to find out about ulex and other obscure 'U' words.
Frankie helps make sense of the bewildering times we live in with the help of Sara Pascoe, Katherine Ryan, Mona Chalabi and a variety of guests.
Open Mic: Hit sitcom that's packed with witticism-packed weirdness. Patrick hosts an open mic night to lure customers into the store, and Alexis works on a marketing strategy for the motel.
The Barbecue: Hot comedy from across the pond. The Rose family throws a barbecue to celebrate David and Patrick's four-month anniversary, but an unexpected visitor threatens everything.
The Jazzaguy: Sparklingly showy silliness with the hit sitcom. Johnny is warmly embraced by the Jazzagals, David and Stevie take a brief holiday, and Alexis goes out with Ted's rival.
Shopping from Home.
Shopping from Home.
Game: The bikers continue their culinary journey through time as they celebrate British food. Time for some meaty dishes with rabbit, grouse and venison on the menu.
Police in Surrey are on the case of a violent gang who steal millions of pounds worth of jewellery, and Avon and Somerset police tackle guns being sent through the post from Miami.
The National Crime Agency - Britain's answer to the FBI - tackle a people-smuggling ring, a Ghanaian cocaine smuggling operation, and organised online romance fraud.
The Stakes Are High: In Perth, the pressure is on for a team tackling a high-stakes engine rebuild, and in Townsville, a crew of experts build a massive underframe for a freight powerhouse.
Monterey to San Diego: Rick Stein travels down the Californian coastline immortalised by John Steinbeck, past citrus groves and vineyards that inspired one of his favourite films, and into LA.
Officers hand over a convict to Mexican authorities in the middle of the Juarez Lincoln bridge, and a man drops a marijuana joint on a customs floor in Progreso.
A man fears for his life after being stopped by a cartel in Laredo, clubbers in Detroit are found with drugs disguised in liquid, and officers stop a shipment of frozen rats.
Guy Martin helps with the restoration of Birmingham Botanical Gardens while learning how to blow glass and trying to rebuild one of the first lawnmowers.
The rucking great motoring show. Jeremy and James enjoy a game of rugby without leaving their driving seats, and Lewis Hamilton swaps his F1 wheels for a Reasonably Priced Car.
More motoring mayhem. Jeremy and Richard tackle the interconnected issues of an ageing population and the complexity of modern cars by designing a vehicle for pensioners.
Food Pairings: Si and Dave explore how British ingredients can combine to make classic pairings. The duo create a pork and apple dish and a pear and stilton tart.
San Diego to Baja California: Rick enjoys a hearty steak-and-eggs American diner special before heading across the reputedly busiest border crossing in the world - San Diego to Tijuana.
More Unseen Bits: Rob Brydon, Lee Mack and David Mitchell return for another episode of previously unseen material with guests including Charlie Brooker, Johnny Marr and Shazia Mirza.
Richard is joined by comedian Maisie Adam, satirical impressionist Rory Bremner, Olympic gold medal-winning rower James Cracknell, and actor Michelle Gayle on this edition of the show.
Our survey says... that former Family Fortunes host Les Dennis is joining actor Charlie Brooks, comedian Darren Harriott, and TV presenter Melinda Messenger on this edition of the show.

programmes.show_name: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programmes.show_description: Team captains Paul Merton and Ian Hislop are joined by a variety of guest hosts and panellists from the worlds of comedy, journalism and current affairs for a look back at 2022.
New. Frankie is joined by Sara Pascoe, Katherine Ryan, Mona Chalabi and a variety of other guests as he continues to try to make sense of the bewildering times we live in.
Ulex: Sandi Toksvig is joined by Bridget Christie, Rosie Jones, Cariad Lloyd and Alan Davies to find out about ulex and other obscure 'U' words.
Dara O Briain and co embrace Corbynmania as they lampoon the Labour leadership contest. Also on the ballot are Ed Gamble, Milton Jones, Katherine Ryan and Josh Widdicombe.
You wouldn't let it lie! Bob Mortimer guests on the very witty panel show, where fact and fiction entwine. Gabby Logan, Katherine Parkinson and Steve Backshall also appear.
Knees and Knockers: A knowledgeable knees-up as Stephen Fry tests Sara Pascoe, Jack Whitehall, David Mitchell and Alan Davies on their know-how of things beginning with 'K'.
Frankie is joined by Sara Pascoe, Katherine Ryan, Mona Chalabi and a variety of other guests as he continues to try to make sense of the bewildering times we live in.
The Olive Branch: More genial humour from the backwater town. David bids to rekindle his romance with Patrick, Moira steals Alexis's proposal, and Johnny gives a useless gift to Stevie.
Baby Sprinkle: Further grown-up comedy with a deliciously farcical taste. Jocelyn bullies David into throwing a last-minute baby shower for her, and Alexis runs into an old friend.
The Rollout: The sitcom that's almost criminal to miss! Moira tries to get out of jury duty as David runs damage control for a Rose Apothecary product.
Shopping from Home.
Shopping from Home.
Spices: The bikers turn up the heat, exploring the origins of Britain's favourite spices. They also visit a chilli festival, with mouth-numbing consequences.
A Surrey police response unit is called to a stabbing incident at a teen house party in a captivating insight into teamwork, tactics and compassion.
The National Crime Agency take on a people-trafficking group while Surrey and City of London Police hunt down exploitative romance fraudsters.
Cane Train Rescue: A locomotive needs a quick double bogie change to get back to the sugarcane harvest. Meanwhile, the heat is on for a tricky air conditioner replacement.
San Diego to Baja California: Rick enjoys a hearty steak-and-eggs American diner special before heading across the reputedly busiest border crossing in the world - San Diego to Tijuana.
Border officers in Detroit apprehend a woman after finding a handgun and ammunition in her vehicle, and officers in Eagle Pass find 150,000 dollars' worth of methamphetamine.
Officers find a man-made compartment within the trailer of a truck returning Stateside, and two Americans arouse suspicions with an unsafe lane change at the US-Canada border.
Britain's Fastest Cyclist: The motoring fan attempts, with the help of slipstreaming, to break the British record for outright speed on a bicycle with the help of Olympian Laura Trott.
The humble motoring show that's now a worldwide hit. It's boat vs car as Jeremy Clarkson and James May vie for bragging rights in a tense race up the stunning New Zealand coast.
Taxi for Hammond. Richard locates the world's best cab, Clarkson tests out the Ferrari F12 and film director Ron Howard is the star in the Reasonably Priced Car. Happy days!
Cheese: The bikers explore how Britain's love affair with cheese has evolved over centuries, and discover that the original cheesecake recipes were British.

Guadalajara to Puerto Vallarta: Leaving the Baja Peninsula, Rick explores Mexico's western mainland and visits the town that put tequila - and the country itself - on the world map: Guadalajara. S1 Ep
At Christmas: Rob Brydon hosts a festive edition with David Mitchell joined by Victoria Coren Mitchell and Naga Munchetty while Lee Mack is joined by Alex Brooker and Melvyn Hayes.
Richard is joined by comedian Maisie Adam, satirical impressionist Rory Bremner, Olympic gold medal-winning rower James Cracknell, and actor Michelle Gayle on this edition of the show.
Our survey says... that former Family Fortunes host Les Dennis is joining actor Charlie Brooks, comedian Darren Harriott, and TV presenter Melinda Messenger on this edition of the show.
Knees and Knockers: A knowledgeable knees-up as Stephen Fry tests Sara Pascoe, Jack Whitehall, David Mitchell and Alan Davies on their know-how of things beginning with 'K'.
New. As mankind continues to hit snooze on the Doomsday Clock, Frankie Boyle helps make sense of things with the help of Sara Pascoe, Katherine Ryan, Mona Chalabi and special guests.

programmes.show_name: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programmes.show_description: Clive Myrie guest hosts as writer and comedian Andy Hamilton and journalist Helen Lewis join team captains Paul Merton and Ian Hislop to delve into the news.
Jeremy Corbyn's landslide win hogs the headlines as Ed Byrne, Nathan Caton, Ed Gamble and Sara Pascoe join the regulars to deride his dress sense - oh, and the week's news.
